reasoning: The Brooklyn Nets entered a full rebuild in the 2024 offseason, trading their best player, Mikal Bridges, for draft picks. They also reacquired their 2025 and 2026 first-round picks, strongly incentivizing a "tanking" strategy for the 2024-25 season to target a top draft prospect like Cooper Flagg. Even if they draft a generational talent in 2025, or use their projected 2025 cap space and draft assets to acquire stars, a one-year turnaround to go from a lottery team to winning the fiercely competitive Eastern Conference (against established teams like the Celtics, Knicks, and 76ers) in 2025-26 is incredibly unlikely. While a "superteam" assembly is theoretically possible, historical precedent makes the chances of this happening and immediately succeeding less than 1%. Therefore, I am forecasting at the allowed minimum of 0.02.
